issue_title: Add support for SyncJumpWidth to the CAN Network configuration
issue_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

I have a can device [PCAN-miniPCIE FD](https://www.peak-system.com/PCAN-miniPCIe-FD.464.0.html?&L=1 
) which for my network requires the SyncJumpWidth to be configured properly (based on the base clock frequency the default calculated by socketcan is too small of a time slice). At present I cannot configure the sync jump width automatically at bringup using a networkd file, and the can bus is flakey as a result.

The following documentation indicates the available settings in systemd.network: https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man5/systemd.network.5.html#%5BCAN%5D_SECTION_OPTIONS


**Describe the solution you'd like**
A clear and concise description of what you want to happen.

- Adding an additional configuration option for `SyncJumpWidth` to the configuration file format. Add TQ, Seg1, Seg2, and PropSeg as well. 
- For my application setting the SamplePoint and allowing the calculation to be automatic and then setting just the SJW manually is acceptable. However, setting all manually would be acceptable (and more correct).

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

**The systemd version you checked that didn't have the feature you are asking for**
<!-- If this is not the most recently released upstream version, then please check first if it has that feature already. -->
`systemd 245 (245.4-4ubuntu3.4)`
I also looked through the master branch for `SyncJumpWidth`, `sync jump width` and `sjw`.

I looked at this issue, but it did not seem like it made the bit timing configuration directly accessible. https://github.com/systemd/systemd/issues/15219
pr_title: network: introduce several CAN interface related settings
